content:
# Wasia Formation

**Summary**: A Middle Cretaceous clastic unit in central Saudi Arabia.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995]] studied the Wasia formation to evaluate its hydrogeological properties.

- [[2019-ahmed-2|Ahmed, 2019]] characterized the hydrochemistry of the wasia-formation.

## Depositional Environment

- [[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995]] alluvial to near-shore marine deltaic environments

- [[2019-ahmed-2|Ahmed, 2019]] characterized the hydrochemistry of the wasia-formation.

## Reservoir Properties



## Diagenetic History

- [[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995]] ferrugination, kaolinitization, and carbonate cementation

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The formation is a significant aquifer providing part of the Riyadh fresh water supply. ([[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995, p. 24]])

- The Wasia aquifer is a prolific source of groundwater for Riyadh, with high nitrate and sulfate concentrations observed. ([[2019-ahmed-2|Ahmed, 2019, p. 22]])
